rulings.Case Title: ARDELLA FOX VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Re: Plaintiffs' Motion For Preference In Setting Case For Trial And Extending Discovery Cut-Off Consistent With The March 20, 2018 Appellate Opinion
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Trial Motion Calendar for Monday, March 26, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. The parties shall appear in Department 503 at 11:15a.m. to discuss the proposed ruling on plaintiffs' motion for preference: Pursuant to the March 20, 2018 opinion filed by the First District Court of Appeal, plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cut-off, filed November 17, 2017, is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. § 36(a). The November 5, 2018 trial date is advanced to June 25, 2018 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Based on the March 20, 2018 appellate opinion, the last day pursuant to C.C.P. § 36(f) is July 18, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 21, 2018. At the hearing the Court will inquire whether the parties will stipulate to shortened notice for summary judgment/adjudication motions. If so, the notice period and briefing schedule will be determined. If not, the Court will assign a trial date closer to the full 120 days and the below dates and deadlines will be amended. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this order or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Fact discovery cut-off date is June 8, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is June 22, 2018. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than April 13, 2018. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. (Tentative ruling Continued, see next entry)
